title: Displayed FSM output values are not very useful
body:
From one of [your answers](http://stackoverflow.com/a/32648163/2698019):

> Note: The FSM outputs in this case are not directly the four ``O`` signal bits. Instead Yosys created an FSM with a three bit output and an encoder outside of the FSM for creating the four ``O`` signal bits.

That's OK, but when exporting a FSM as a kiss2 file I am expecting to see the actual outputs. Do I get it right that currently there is no way to do that? And if not, then what's the idea of having a kiss2 file with these half-backed output values?
